text.title: Fixed-Price Risk Loading Model Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Fixed-Price Risk Loading Model application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every buffer line traces to a risk register entry.

rationale.source: Fixed-Price Risk Loading Model authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Buffers without anchors invite negotiation pressure; anchored buffers defend.

rule.statement: Risks classified in closed tiers (catastrophic / high / medium / low / accepted).

rationale.source: Fixed-Price Risk Loading Model authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Open severity strings collapse; tiers make the loading auditable.

rule.statement: Loading ratios calibrated against historical actuals; deviations >25% require justification.

rationale.source: Fixed-Price Risk Loading Model authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Anchored loading hides over- or under-pricing; calibration keeps the model honest.

rule.statement: Bid owner named; second-review on margin <10% gross.

rationale.source: Fixed-Price Risk Loading Model authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Plural ownership lets margin slip; second-review catches under-priced bids.

rule.statement: Model carries semver + last_reviewed; ratio update triggers a version bump.

rationale.source: Fixed-Price Risk Loading Model authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Frozen models go stale; versioning enables longitudinal cost defence.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.
